response.justification: Candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering capabilities and fintech exposure through RIDE Capital, but critically lacks the mandatory technical foundations for this mid-senior capital markets role. C++ experience totals approximately 6 months at National Instruments, substantially below the required 5–10 years on Windows systems. No evidence of SQL Server/Oracle expertise, systems-level programming, capital markets platform development, or fixed income domain knowledge. Career trajectory is overwhelmingly web/backend (Node.js, TypeScript, React) rather than the low-latency, systems-level engineering needed for capital markets infrastructure.
strengths: Demonstrated fintech industry exposure through RIDE Capital work reshaping investment practices
Excellent communication and teaching capabilities evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source community engagement
Proven ability to rapidly learn diverse technologies and adapt across domains (robotics, GIS, web, mobile, embedded systems)
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ experience (~6 months professionally vs. 5–10 years required) with no evidence of Windows platform systems development
Generic SQL knowledge with no demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle expertise, nor capital markets database architecture experience
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking systems domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (6 months vs. 5–10 years)
